name: Customer accepts the quote on the public page
description: From the public quote page, customer taps Accept. They see a confirmation, and the contractor sees the status flip to 'won' and a notification appears.

persona.name: Asha Patel
persona.context: Read the quote, happy with the price, ready to commit.

preconditions: Public quote page loaded for an unaccepted quote
Diego (contractor) has dashboard open in another tab

steps.expect: Confirmation step (small modal): 'Confirm acceptance — total $430.00. Diego will be in touch.' with Accept and Cancel
Page updates to 'Accepted ✓' state with a friendly thank-you and next-step text ('Diego will text you to schedule')
Diego's /quotes shows the quote in decided-won; live activity pill on /dashboard shows 'Patel accepted your quote'
Push/SMS/email per Diego's preferences within ~30s; bell counter increments

evaluationCriteria.check: Accepted state feels celebratory but not gaudy
Customer never sees an error after Accept — even if the network blips, retry is invisible
Double-tapping Accept doesn't double-submit; idempotent accept endpoint
Confirmation modal traps focus and has a labelled close button
